commit_msg: fix: pass db-path to ytpipeline from render_all, add --db-path flag
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Handoff
subtitle: He built a bridge so the pipeline could find its way home—if it remembered where home was.
when_where: 🕐 1:20 PM, still on main, still in the same terminal session. 📝 The isolation was done. Now came the real work: making sure ytpipeline had what it needed to survive outside the parent's protection. The db-path had to move like contraband, hand to hand, function to function.
who_whom: 🔍 render_all held the database path—the one piece of truth that mattered. 🗂️ The ytpipeline child process needed it, but couldn't ask the parent directly anymore. Omkar had to architect the handoff: a --db-path flag, explicit and undeniable.
what_why: ⚙️ He added the flag. He threaded the needle. Now render_all could hand the db-path to ytpipeline like a dying man passing his wallet to his son. 💻 No more guessing. No more inheritance of poison. Each process knew exactly where its data lived. 🧠 It was clean. It was deliberate. It was the kind of fix that only works if you've already accepted that the original design was rotten.
verdict: He didn't trust the system to work; he just made sure it couldn't lie about why it failed.
